# [AArch64] Wrong code at -O1

**Author:** XChy
**URL:** https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/180409
**Status:** Closed
**Labels:** miscompilation, llvm:SelectionDAG, generated by fuzzer, regression:22
**Closed Date:** 2026-02-12T09:58:31Z

## Body

Reproducer: https://godbolt.org/z/WcKs34b95
Testcase:
```c
#include <stdint.h>
#include <stdio.h>
#define BS_VEC(type, num) type __attribute__((vector_size(num * sizeof(type))))
int main()
{
    BS_VEC(uint32_t, 4) BS_VAR_0 = {};
    uint64_t BS_CHECKSUM = 0;
BS_LABEL_0:
    BS_VAR_0 = __builtin_shufflevector(
        (BS_VEC(uint32_t, 16)){ 4, 4, 4, 4, 4 },
        (BS_VEC(uint32_t, 16)){ 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 4 }
            * __builtin_shufflevector(BS_VAR_0, BS_VAR_0, 4, 7, 0, 0, 3, 0, 1,
                                      7, 3, 0, 6, 2, 6, 2, 3, 4),
        2, 4, 31, 24);
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < 4; i++) BS_CHECKSUM ^= BS_VAR_0[i];
    int volatile a = 0;
    if (a) goto BS_LABEL_0;
    printf("BackSmith Checksum = 0x%016llx\n", BS_CHECKSUM);
}
```

Output at -O0:
```
BackSmith Checksum = 0x0000000000000000
```
Output at -O1:
```
BackSmith Checksum = 0x0000000000000004
```

<details>
Reproducer: https://godbolt.org/z/WcKs34b95
Testcase:
```c
#include &lt;stdint.h&gt;
#include &lt;stdio.h&gt;
#define BS_VEC(type, num) type __attribute__((vector_size(num * sizeof(type))))
int main()
{
    BS_VEC(uint32_t, 4) BS_VAR_0 = {};
    uint64_t BS_CHECKSUM = 0;
BS_LABEL_0:
    BS_VAR_0 = __builtin_shufflevector(
        (BS_VEC(uint32_t, 16)){ 4, 4, 4, 4, 4 },
        (BS_VEC(uint32_t, 16)){ 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 4 }
            * __builtin_shufflevector(BS_VAR_0, BS_VAR_0, 4, 7, 0, 0, 3, 0, 1,

        2, 4, 31, 24);
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i &lt; 4; i++) BS_CHECKSUM ^= BS_VAR_0[i];
    int volatile a = 0;
    if (a) goto BS_LABEL_0;
    printf("BackSmith Checksum = 0x%016llx\n", BS_CHECKSUM);
}
```

Output at -O0:
```
BackSmith Checksum = 0x0000000000000000
```
Output at -O1:
```
BackSmith Checksum = 0x0000000000000004
```
</details>

### Comment 2 - abhishek-kaushik22

Clang 21.1.0 produces the correct results at `-O1`. 
The opt-bisect points to `sroa` pass on main https://godbolt.org/z/qq17MveYY

---

### Comment 3 - XChy

@abhishek-kaushik22, thanks for your investigation. I run the IR after SROA with `lli`:
```llvm
@.str = private unnamed_addr constant [32 x i8] c"BackSmith Checksum = 0x%016llx\0A\00", align 1

define dso_local i32 @main() {
entry:
  %a = alloca i32, align 4
  br label %BS_LABEL_0

BS_LABEL_0:
  %BS_CHECKSUM.0 = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %BS_CHECKSUM.1, %for.cond.cleanup ]
  %BS_VAR_0.0 = phi <4 x i32> [ zeroinitializer, %entry ], [ %shuffle2, %for.cond.cleanup ]
  %shuffle = shufflevector <4 x i32> %BS_VAR_0.0, <4 x i32> %BS_VAR_0.0, <16 x i32> <i32 4, i32 7, i32 0, i32 0, i32 3, i32 0, i32 1, i32 7, i32 3, i32 0, i32 6, i32 2, i32 6, i32 2, i32 3, i32 4>
  %mul = mul <16 x i32> <i32 0, i32 0, i32 0, i32 0, i32 0, i32 0, i32 0, i32 0, i32 4, i32 0, i32 0, i32 0, i32 0, i32 0, i32 0, i32 0>, %shuffle
  %shuffle2 = shufflevector <16 x i32> <i32 4, i32 4, i32 4, i32 4, i32 4, i32 0, i32 0, i32 0, i32 0, i32 0, i32 0, i32 0, i32 0, i32 0, i32 0, i32 0>, <16 x i32> %mul, <4 x i32> <i32 2, i32 4, i32 31, i32 24>
  br label %for.cond

for.cond:
  %BS_CHECKSUM.1 = phi i64 [ %BS_CHECKSUM.0, %BS_LABEL_0 ], [ %xor, %for.body ]
  %i.0 = phi i32 [ 0, %BS_LABEL_0 ], [ %inc, %for.body ]
  %cmp = icmp ult i32 %i.0, 4
  br i1 %cmp, label %for.body, label %for.cond.cleanup

for.cond.cleanup:
  store volatile i32 0, ptr %a, align 4
  %a.0. = load volatile i32, ptr %a, align 4
  %tobool = icmp ne i32 %a.0., 0
  br i1 %tobool, label %BS_LABEL_0, label %if.end

for.body:
  %vecext = extractelement <4 x i32> %shuffle2, i32 %i.0
  %conv = zext i32 %vecext to i64
  %xor = xor i64 %BS_CHECKSUM.1, %conv
  %inc = add i32 %i.0, 1
  br label %for.cond

if.end:
  %call = call i32 (ptr, ...) @printf(ptr noundef @.str, i64 noundef %BS_CHECKSUM.1)
  ret i32 0
}

declare void @llvm.lifetime.start.p0(ptr captures(none)) #1

declare void @llvm.lifetime.end.p0(ptr captures(none)) #1

declare noundef i32 @printf(ptr noundef readonly captures(none), ...) #2

!46 = distinct !DIAssignID()
!61 = distinct !DIAssignID()
```

The output is correct. It indicates that the miscompilation happened in the backend instead of the SROA pass.

### Comment 4 - davemgreen

I didn't trace this through exactly, but I would guess it might be an interaction between assuming mul * 0 doesn't demand bits from d79e7dc939a3e9c422ecb4f7de17ca7a6d827ba7 (FYI @RKSimon), and #177578 making undef vector lanes poison (FYI @arsenm).

```
      t5: v16i32 = concat_vectors t2, undef:v4i32, undef:v4i32, undef:v4i32
    t7: v16i32 = vector_shuffle<u,u,u,u,u,u,u,u,3,u,u,u,u,u,u,0> t5, undef:v16i32
    t11: v16i32 = BUILD_VECTOR poison:i32, poison:i32, poison:i32, poison:i32, poison:i32, poison:i32, poison:i32, poison:i32, Constant:i32<4>, poison:i32, poison:i32, 
poison:i32, poison:i32, poison:i32, poison:i32, Constant:i32<0>
  t12: v16i32 = mul t7, t11
t17: i32 = extract_vector_elt t12, Constant:i64<15>
t19: i32 = extract_vector_elt t12, Constant:i64<8>

=>

      t5: v16i32 = concat_vectors t2, undef:v4i32, undef:v4i32, undef:v4i32
    t28: v16i32 = vector_shuffle<u,u,u,u,u,u,u,u,3,u,u,u,u,u,u,u> t5, undef:v16i32
    t11: v16i32 = BUILD_VECTOR poison:i32, poison:i32, poison:i32, poison:i32, poison:i32, poison:i32, poison:i32, poison:i32, Constant:i32<4>, poison:i32, poison:
poison:i32, poison:i32, poison:i32, poison:i32, Constant:i32<0>
  t12: v16i32 = mul t28, t11
t17: i32 = extract_vector_elt t12, Constant:i64<15>
t19: i32 = extract_vector_elt t12, Constant:i64<8>
```
